chapter_context.summary: Solomon's proverb collection begins, contrasting the wise and foolish, the diligent and lazy, the wholesome tongue and the mouth of the wicked in daily life.

outline.text: Proverbs of Solomon: Wisdom vs. Folly; Diligence vs. Laziness
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Proverbs of Solomon: Wisdom vs. Folly; Diligence vs. Laziness (10:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: The Tongue of the Just vs. The Mouth of the Wicked
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Tongue of the Just vs. The Mouth of the Wicked (10:6-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Wealth, Correction, and the Blessing of the LORD that Maketh Rich
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Wealth, Correction, and the Blessing of the LORD that Maketh Rich (10:15-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Hope of the Righteous vs. Expectation and Ruin of the Wicked
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hope of the Righteous vs. Expectation and Ruin of the Wicked (10:23-32), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

key_themes.title: Wisdom vs. Folly
key_themes.description: Proverbs outlines a practical blueprint for daily living, contrasting the actions, words, and consequences of the wise with those of the foolish.

key_themes.title: The Fear of the Lord
key_themes.description: The foundational principle of wisdom is a reverent awe and trust in God, which guides moral choices and shapes character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the practical sayings of Chapter 10, what is one specific area of your speech, relationships, or work where you can apply divine wisdom today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose the path of integrity and diligence over convenience or compromise?

cultural_historical.title: Ancient Near Eastern Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Proverbs shares structural and thematic similarities with other ancient wisdom writings, but stands unique in placing the Fear of Yahweh as the foundation of all true wisdom.
